problem_text: Determine whether sum_{n=1}^infinity (n + 2)/(n^3 + n) converges or diverges.

content.label: Positive-term series
content.applies_when: the terms are positive, so a comparison test applies

content.meaning: sum 1/n^p converges iff p > 1; the comparison series 1/n^2 has p = 2 and converges.
if lim a_n/b_n = c is finite and positive, then sum a_n and sum b_n converge or diverge together.

content.label: for large n the term behaves like n/n^3 = 1/n^2, so pick b_n = 1/n^2
content.action: for large n the term behaves like n/n^3 = 1/n^2, so pick b_n = 1/n^2
content.purpose: select a comparison series matching the dominant growth

content.label: compute lim [(n+2)/(n^3+n)]/[1/n^2] = lim (n^3 + 2n^2)/(n^3 + n) = 1, finite and positive
content.action: compute lim [(n+2)/(n^3+n)]/[1/n^2] = lim (n^3 + 2n^2)/(n^3 + n) = 1, finite and positive
content.purpose: form the limit ratio required by the Limit Comparison Test

content.label: Finite positive limit
content.applies_when: the limit c = 1 is finite and positive
content.transformation: sum a_n and sum b_n share the same convergence behavior

content.label: since sum 1/n^2 converges and c = 1, sum (n + 2)/(n^3 + n) converges
content.action: since sum 1/n^2 converges and c = 1, sum (n + 2)/(n^3 + n) converges
